title: TCP Server — Socket Listening
description: Create a TCPServer class that binds to a host and port, listens for connections, and accepts clients in a loop. Use socket.socket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM) with SO_REUSEADDR. The server should have start() and stop() methods, a host and port attribute, and an is_running property. The start() method should set up the socket and set is_running to True. The stop() method should close the socket and set is_running to False. Include an accept_connections() method that would loop accepting clients (for testing, it just needs to be defined and set up the socket correctly).
test_code:
# Tests for Step 6: TCPServer
import socket

assert 'TCPServer' in dir(), 'TCPServer class must be defined'

# Test constructor stores host and port
server = TCPServer('127.0.0.1', 9876)
assert server.host == '127.0.0.1', 'TCPServer should store host'
assert server.port == 9876, 'TCPServer should store port'

# Test is_running property
assert server.is_running is False, 'Server should not be running initially'

# Test start/stop
assert callable(getattr(server, 'start', None)), 'TCPServer must have start method'
assert callable(getattr(server, 'stop', None)), 'TCPServer must have stop method'

server.start()
assert server.is_running is True, 'Server should be running after start()'

# Test that socket is bound and listening
assert server._socket is not None, 'Server should have a socket after start'
assert server._socket.fileno() != -1, 'Socket should be open'

# Test that we can connect to it
test_client = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
try:
    test_client.connect(('127.0.0.1', 9876))
    connected = True
except ConnectionRefusedError:
    connected = False
finally:
    test_client.close()
assert connected, 'Should be able to connect to the running server'

# Test stop
server.stop()
assert server.is_running is False, 'Server should not be running after stop()'

# Test accept_connections method exists
assert callable(getattr(server, 'accept_connections', None)), 'TCPServer must have accept_connections method'
acceptance_criteria: TCPServer class stores host and port attributes
is_running property returns False initially, True after start(), False after stop()
start() creates a TCP socket with SO_REUSEADDR, binds, and listens
accept_connections() method is defined for handling incoming connections
A client can connect to the server after start() is called
hints: Use socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) to create a TCP socket.
In stop(), close the socket inside a try/except to handle already-closed sockets.
